title: Two friends, one painting
summary: Two friends visit a museum. One loves a painting. The other doesn't understand it.

body.speaker: Lan
body.text: Look, Tom. This painting is amazing.

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: Really? It's just a blue square. My little sister can do that.

body.speaker: Lan
body.text: But look at the blue. It's not one blue. It's many blues.

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: Blue is blue, Lan. I don't see it.

body.speaker: Lan
body.text: Come closer. Look here. And here. Do you see now?

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: Hmm. Maybe. A little. But it's still a square.

body.speaker: Lan
body.text: Yes. But how does it make you feel?

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: Feel? It's a painting. I don't feel anything.

body.speaker: Lan
body.text: When I look at it, I feel quiet. Like the sea at night.

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: Oh. I don't feel that. I feel… nothing.

body.speaker: Lan
body.text: That's okay. Maybe we are not looking at the same painting.

grammarNotes.title: Imperatives
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Câu mệnh lệnh ngắn: 'Look', 'Come closer'. Dùng động từ nguyên thể ở đầu câu.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Short commands use the bare verb: 'Look', 'Come closer'. They are common in spoken English.

blanks.explanation: Lan says the painting is amazing.
Tom says it's a blue square.
Lan suggests they are not looking at the same painting.

translationVi:
Lan: Nhìn này, Tom. Bức tranh này thật tuyệt.
Tom: Thật á? Chỉ là một hình vuông màu xanh. Em gái nhỏ của tớ cũng vẽ được mà.
Lan: Nhưng hãy nhìn màu xanh đi. Không phải một sắc xanh. Là nhiều sắc xanh.
Tom: Xanh là xanh thôi, Lan. Tớ không thấy gì cả.
Lan: Lại gần hơn xem. Nhìn chỗ này. Và chỗ này. Cậu thấy chưa?
Tom: Hmm. Có lẽ. Một chút. Nhưng nó vẫn là hình vuông.
Lan: Đúng vậy. Nhưng nó làm cậu cảm thấy thế nào?
Tom: Cảm thấy á? Là một bức tranh thôi. Tớ chẳng cảm thấy gì.
Lan: Khi tớ nhìn nó, tớ thấy yên bình. Như biển vào ban đêm.
Tom: Ồ. Tớ không cảm thấy thế. Tớ cảm thấy… không gì cả.
Lan: Không sao đâu. Có lẽ chúng ta không nhìn cùng một bức tranh.

questions.explanation: They talk about a painting in front of them.

questions.hint: Who speaks first and is positive?

questions.prompt: What does Tom say about the painting?
What does Lan see in the colour blue?
questions.explanation: 'It's not one blue. It's many blues.'
questions.hint: Lan corrects Tom about the colour.

questions.prompt: Does Tom feel something when he looks at it?
questions.hint: Look at Tom's later answer about feeling.

questions.prompt: What does Lan feel when she looks at the painting?
options: Quiet, like the sea at night
questions.explanation: 'I feel quiet. Like the sea at night.'
questions.hint: Look for a comparison with the sea.

questions.prompt: What does Lan ask Tom to do?
Does Tom change his mind about the painting?
questions.hint: Listen to Tom's reaction after he looks closer.

options: They may not be seeing the same painting
questions.explanation: 'Maybe we are not looking at the same painting.'

options: Two people can look at the same thing and see something different
questions.explanation: Lan and Tom see the same painting in very different ways.
questions.hint: Think about what is shared and what is not.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Lan says 'Maybe we are not looking at the same painting.' What does she really mean? Is it about the painting, or about Lan and Tom?
